additional_metadata.note: Hybrid event, lightning talk format. Presentation titles marked as tentative in source.

events.agenda: 18:30 doors open; 19:00 start with multiple lightning talks on scaling dbt across an organization; ~21:30 wrap-up and networking.
events.event_description: Tokyo dbt Meetup is a networking event open to everyone who works with data. Centered on dbt experience sharing, it covers a wide range of practical topics including analytics engineering, data platforms, modeling, testing, operations, automation, tooling, and team design. This session's theme was 'Spreading dbt across the organization' - how to move dbt from a data team's specialized tool to something the whole organization uses.
events.location: DATUM STUDIO Inc. (Meguro HQ), Meguro Arco Tower 16F, Shimo-Meguro 1-8-1, Meguro City, Tokyo
events.raw_text:
Thu, Jul 2 · 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM JST

Theme: 組織で広げるdbt (Spreading dbt across the organization). LT speakers: 桑原健一 (LELEPONO) on 5 years of HR data maintenance with dbt; 和田裕己 (Sansan) on company-wide dbt core rollout; 新垣泰弘 (newmo) on 'quitting being the data gatekeeper'.

talks.description: A 5-year retrospective on building out HR data infrastructure with dbt from scratch, despite a lack of knowledge, people, APIs, databases, and budget.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 桑原 健一 / 株式会社LELEPONO/ 代表取締役「知見・人・API・DB・予算 ─ ナイナイ尽くしだった人事データ整備 with dbt、5年間の学び」
talks.title: 5 Years of Learnings Building HR Data Infrastructure with dbt, Starting with Nothing

talks.description: The journey of rolling out and popularizing dbt core company-wide at Sansan.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 和田 裕己 / Sansan株式会社 / データエンジニア「dbt coreの全社展開〜普及までの道のり」（仮）
talks.title: The Path to Company-Wide Rollout and Adoption of dbt Core

talks.description: On stepping back from being the sole 'gatekeeper' of data and instead designing operations that delegate authority to end users.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 新垣 泰弘 / newmo株式会社 / データエンジニア「データの番人をやめる」
talks.title: Quitting Being the Data Gatekeeper

additional_metadata.note: Hybrid event, lightning talk format. Presentation titles marked as tentative in source.

events.agenda: 18:30 doors open; 19:00 start with lightning talks on moving dbt from 'writing' to 'operating' in production; ~21:30 wrap-up and networking.
events.event_description: Tokyo dbt Meetup is a networking event open to everyone who works with data, covering analytics engineering, data platforms, modeling, testing, operations, automation, tooling, and team design. This session's theme was moving dbt from just 'writing' SQL models to 'running,' 'protecting,' and 'evolving' it in production - covering execution/control, resilience to schema changes, testing/permissions design, and CLI/automation/agent tooling around dbt.
events.location: DATUM STUDIO Inc. (Meguro HQ), Meguro Arco Tower 16F, Shimo-Meguro 1-8-1, Meguro City, Tokyo
events.raw_text:
Wed, Feb 25 · 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM JST

Theme: dbtは「書く」から「動かす」へ (from writing dbt to operating it). LT speakers: 古田裕規 (Mitsumore) on building an AI agent that made him appreciate dbt more; しめにゃん (Knowledge Work) on automating DB metadata into the data mart; 高橋裕人 (Nowcast) on graduating from running dbt-core 'vaguely'; よしけん (commmune) on streamlining DB ingestion and cross-product dbt integration.

talks.description: How building an internal AI agent for data engineering tasks made the speaker appreciate dbt's value even more.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 古田 裕規 / 株式会社ミツモア / Data Group Manager「データエンジニア AI Agentを作ったらdbtのありがたみが増した」（仮）
talks.title: Building a Data Engineer AI Agent Made Me Appreciate dbt Even More

talks.description: How an app development team came to write DB metadata, carry it through to the data mart, and fully automate the process.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: しめにゃん / 株式会社ナレッジワーク / ソフトウェアエンジニア「アプリ開発チームがDBのメタデータを書く。それをデータマートまで持ち込む。全てを自動化したい！→しました」（仮）
talks.title: How an App Dev Team Wrote DB Metadata, Brought It to the Data Mart, and Automated Everything

talks.description: Moving beyond running dbt-core 'by feel' by analyzing query logs and adding control on the data warehouse side.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: 高橋 裕人 / 株式会社ナウキャスト / データエンジニア「dbtを『なんとなく動かす』を卒業する 〜クエリログから紐解くdbt-coreの実態とDWH側での制御〜」
talks.title: Graduating from Running dbt 'Vaguely' - Understanding dbt-core Through Query Logs and DWH-Side Control

talks.description: Practical approaches to streamlining DB data ingestion and integrating dbt across multiple products.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: よしけん / コミューン株式会社 / シニアデータエンジニア「DB取り込みの省力化と、複数プロダクトをまたぐdbt連携の実践」
talks.title: Streamlining DB Ingestion and Cross-Product dbt Integration in Practice

talks.description: An overview of the latest dbt updates, including a new engine with SQL parsing (SDF integration), visual editing, and cost optimization.

events.event_description: Part of the 'dbt Labs on dbt' series, showcasing behind-the-scenes of how dbt Labs uses its own product. This session covered how dbt Labs' internal data team manages its first dbt project, including a deep dive on incremental model upgrades as the project grew from ~11 contributors (pre-2020) to ~92 contributors.
events.event_name: dbt Labs主催のdbt Meetupシリーズ | dbt Labs on dbt' Meetup: Tokyo
events.location: DATUM STUDIO Inc., Toranomon Twin Building East Tower 12F, Toranomon 2-10-1, Minato City, Tokyo
events.raw_text:
Thu, Jul 4, 2024 · 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM JST

Talk by Andrew Escay (dbt Labs, Sr. Data Analyst, Product): 'Incremental Model Upgrades' - how dbt Labs' internal data team manages its own dbt project, which grew from ~11 to ~92 contributors, and enhancements to incremental model materializations for managing large legacy models.

talks.description: A behind-the-scenes look at how dbt Labs' internal data team manages its own dbt project and the tooling built to handle incremental model upgrades as the project scaled from about 11 to 92 contributors.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: プレゼンテーションの詳細：「インクリメンタルモデルアップグレード」このセッションでは、dbt Labsのプロダクト担当シニアデータアナリストであるアンドリューが、dbtプロジェクト管理用に社内で構築した最初のツールについて説明します。
talks.speaker_title: Senior Data Analyst, Product, dbt Labs
talks.title: Incremental Model Upgrades

events.agenda: 12:00 doors, community intro, then three talks on MetricFlow and the semantic/metrics layer landscape.
events.event_description: Tokyo dbt Meetup is a networking event open to everyone who works with data, covering analytics engineering, data stacks, data management, modeling, testing, and team structure. This session's theme was studying dbt-metricflow ahead of its preview release, covering an intro to MetricFlow, why dbt_metrics was deprecated in favor of dbt-metricflow, and a comparison of metrics-layer tools.

events.agenda: 11:50 community intro, then four talks on dbt adoption stories, then Q&A after each.
events.event_description: Tokyo dbt Meetup is a networking event open to everyone who works with data, covering analytics engineering, data stacks, data management, modeling, testing, and team structure. This session featured four adoption-story talks: introducing dbt into an ad reporting platform, adopting dbt as a one-person effort, migrating from raw SQL to dbt, and unit testing a dbt package.
